item.title: Mexico fan loses his job for being a douche
item.description: https://sports.yahoo.com/articles/mexico-fan-loses-job-making-091607440.html We all remember this douche right? He apologized but how sincere can that apology really be? He even had that smug smile on his face after like it was a good joke or something.

item.dc:creator: MissDeeMeanor

item.title: It's real..
item.description: Yes, Sesame Street featured a parody character named Donald Grump. He made multiple appearances on the show, notably as a wealthy, scheming Grouch with a poofy orange wig who famously declared that his name was "on every piece of trash in town". The show has a long history of spoofing the real estate mogul and television personality across several decades: Ronald Grump (1988): A scheming landlord Muppet appeared in a storyline to build "Grump Tower" and tried to evict Oscar the Grouch. Ronald Grump (1994): The character was portrayed in human form by actor Joe Pesci, who wore orange makeup and a lookalike wig in an anniversary special. Donald Grump (2005): At the height of The Apprentice's popularity, the show featured a "Donald Grump" Muppet. He arrived on Sesame Street to commission an apprentice, only to instantly "fire" Elmo for doing a good job.
item.dc:creator: FuManLu

item.title: Beauty Captured # 5 - 75 Amazing Vintage Images!
item.description:
En route to New Orleans, 1972 (photo by William Eggleston)
Aubervilliers, France, 1947 (photo by Louis Stettner)
Sailors watch as aircraft takes off, 1943 (photo by Fons Iannelli)
Barbara through window, 1956 (photo by Wynn Bullock)
Built in 657 AD - Whitby Abbey, Yorkshire, England, circa 1900
Fish Kiss, 1985 (photo by Yuri Dojc)
I usually do Airborne Humans images, but I do run across many double exposure photos that have such a dreamy beautiful effect that I wanted to share Double Exposure # 1 - Family on hilltop and the Wertach Pool, Germany, 1952
West Midlands Safari Park, UK, 1990 (photo by Martin Parr)
Paris, 1948 (photo by Rene Groebli)
Spinning carnival ride, long exposure, 1971 (photo by Roger Vail)
Dublin, Ireland, 1994 (photo by Sam Abell for National Geographic)
Mr. Perkin's Pierce Arrow, NYC, 1946 (photo by Todd Webb)
Still Life, Marseilles, 1992 (photo by Joel-Peter Witkin)
Mount Adams incline, Cincinnati, OH, 1915
Great Wall of China, hand painted lantern slide, 1930
Double Exposure # 2 - Las Vegas, 1950s
NYC, 1943 (photo by Brett Weston)
British parachute jumpers, 1937
Albanian woman, Ellis Island, NYC, 1905 (photo by Lewis W. Hine)
After-Dinner Games, NYC, 1947 (photo by Irving Penn)
Crowds gather on the beach next to the smoldering wreck of the Morro Castle, Asbury Park, NJ, 1934
Atlas Moth, 1990 (photo by Keith Carter)
Plastic dummies in formation on the set of the film "Waterloo", 1970
Ipanema beach, Rio, Brazil, 1978
Tipi (teepee) camp near Pine Ridge, SD, 1891 (photo by John C.H. Grabil)
Egg in spotlight, 1943 (photo by Paul Outerbridge)
Night view of Vancouver from Grouse Mountain, British Columbia, 1958 (photo by David Boyer)
Double Exposure # 3 - New Year's Eve, 1953
My Little Bird, 1928 (photo by Atelier Manassé)
Seconds before landing, parachute jump, 1931 (photo by Willi Ruge)
Spanish Harlem girl with puppy, NYC, 1983 (photo by Joseph Rodriguez)
Suicidal, Mexico City, 1971 (photo by Enrique Metinides)
Austin, Texas, 1974 (photo by Garry Winogrand)
Mardi Gras, royal chariot with Rex at Canal Street ferry, New Orleans, LA, 1906
Watersmeet in Devon, England, circa 1865
Flamenco dancer, La Fonda Hotel, Santa Fe, NM, 1975 (photo by Nick DeWolf)
Soap box brothers, Georgia, 1937 (photo by John Gutmann)
Double Exposure # 4 - Milk Depot, 1930s
Aerial of Diana Ross concert in Central Park, NYC, 1983 (photo by Thomas Hoepker)
Hair, Los Angeles, CA, 1972 (photo by Gary Krueger)
Peasant family, Florence, Italy, 1900 (photo by Fratelli Alinari Bros.)
Skeleton dancers and orchestra, Sydney, Aus, 1928
Father and baby, West Africa, 1950 (photo by Seydou Keïta)
Reppen, Norway, 1898 (photo by Nils Olsson)
The Lodgers, photo montage, 1962 (photos by Robert Doisneau)
Illegal drag racing in the L.A. River Basin, Los Angeles, CA, 1957
Double Exposure # 5 - Girl and cat, 1965
Magician Baron LaVelle and his family at home, Louisville, KY, 1962 (photo by Stern J. Bramson)
The photographer's wife, Rita, on their honeymoon in Paris, 1952 (photo by René Groebli)
Making out in front of the Delta Tau Delta mummy at the University of the South Sewanee, TN, 1940 (photo by Alfred Eisenstaedt)
Baptism in Morehead, KY, 1940 (photo by Marion Post Wolcott)
Blue toy car, Palm Springs, CA, 1960 (photo by Robert Doisneau)
The loop on the Darjeeling Himalayan Railway, India, 1880 (photo by Bourne & Shepherd)
Entrance of the Niagara Falls Suspension bridge - no marching, no bands, no speeding or stopping, 1859
This ship is the Falls of Halladale, wrecked on the coast of Victoria, Australia - accident due to fog and navigational error, 1908
Double Exposure # 6 - Boy, sun and clouds
Yankee Stadium opening day, NYC, 1923
Woman in bed under satin sheets, 1933 (photo by Paul Outerbridge Jr.)
The Sibley coal breaker building, Old Forge, PA, 1866
Franklin & Eddy Streets, San Francisco, CA, 1947 (photo by Max Yavno)
The couple, 1938 (photo by Ben Shahn)
Fork in the road, Ohio, 1973 (photo by Clark Winter)
Varanasi, India - Vishnu temples on the Ganges River, 1866 (photo by Samuel Bourne)
The couple, 1956 (photo by Gordon Parks)
Double Exposure # 7 - Leaf Heads
The farm, 1978 (photo by Sage Sohier)
Volusia County, FL, 1904
Chicago, 1952 (photo by Harry Callahan)
A diver at the River Seine, Paris, 1945 (photo by Robert Doisneau)
Slave cabins, Evergreen Plantation, Wallace, LA, 1948 (photo by Clarence John Laughlin)
Klimsen Chapel on the Klimsenhorn summit, Mount Pilatus, Switzerland, 1960s
Vogue magazine, 1945 (photo by George Platt Lynes)
Double Exposure # 8 - Baby, woman & covered wagon, 1960s
Abandoned Morris Minor car, Dingle Peninsula, County Kerry, Ireland, 1982 (photo by Martin Parr)
A cat in a knot in a tree
item.dc:creator: FAUST44

item.title: Know Your Enemy
item.description:
I’ve got no patience now
So sick of complacence now
Sick of sick of sick of sick of you
Time has come to pay…
Know your enemy!
Yes I know my enemies
They’re the teachers who taught me to fight me
Compromise, conformity, assimilation, submission
Ignorance, hypocrisy, brutality...the elite.
item.dc:creator: hufflesnuff

item.title: Memes vol 707
item.description:
Me because my nose has been bleeding for like 3 days now
Worst book I’ve ever read because of the gore which is surprising because I’m into *a********* (but not like… sexually)
I had an intense interest with the donner party when I was 10 years old. Is it the fact their name so closely resembles dinner party? Who knows
Bruce tax
Gertie tax
Nessie tax

item.title: He’s Wasting Away
item.description: If a MAGAt tries to tell you how manly and strong tRump is, ask them if he’s more masculine than they are and watch the squirming and back-pedaling begin. Will they admit tRump is less of a man’s man than they are or that he’s more masculine?
item.dc:creator: Resistance1000

item.title: Putting on a Corset After 30…
item.description:
Me when I pull out my old cosplays from my cosplay wear house to organize and see what I can still use…
For those curious about an update from yesterday’s vid… It’s embarrassing but if I don’t admit publicly I dropped my own phone on my face and gave myself a black eye, how else can I make fun of myself for the rest of my life… https://imgur.com/gallery/does-this-count-tt-UmhyCcZ
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley
